w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Removed duplicated page fields, added subclassification document indicator, updated field descriptions, simplified image naming convention.
Small edit to verbiage for the description.
Updated TOC, converted error and driver specification to JSON, update naming convention presentation, added address to driver file to support RCN requirements, provided error listing.
Corrected XML naming convention, added Batch CreationTime, added file size to nack, converted ack to JSON and included “header” information.
Added summary info to driver file.
Corrected typos, added resubmission indicator to incoming data transmission, added reconciliation acknowledgement, added transaction count to ACK.
Corrected formatting, table layout, inconsistencies, typos and requirement traceability.
Corrected other type error code listing from 100 to 400 matching the specified range.
Restructured package model to meet transaction model, removed resubmission indicator, updated summary information.
Added a new optional reserved field for trading partner use only. The format_version will now reflect the current specification version.
Removed CheckStub classification, removed the site identifier from driver file naming convention.

Added version to the specification document filename and XSD schema, added acknowledgement package transmission specification details, corrected bookmark links, updated error listing, removed data tag and location from Error Source.
Added timestamp to driver file transmission. Added missing GCI fields.
Transmission file specifications for sending data between the trading partner and Bank of America. All files to be sent using Data Transmission Services (DTS

This driver file includes a listing of all Bank of America clients’ lockboxes and addresses that may accept digital payments via this process. Those that do not accept digital transmissions must follow the existing paper route. Client lockbox addresses are printed on remittance invoices and their websites.
Data to be provided to the trading partner using a JSON file.
Package of data to be sent to Bank of America and placed on the DTS
will be established to mimic mail time delay. Bank of America will ingest files upon receipt for downstream processing.
Each transmission may contain one or more transactions. A transaction represents all the documents normally contained in an envelope. There will be a limit of 150 transactions
A compressed archive containing the images and transaction data files. Fields one and two are the Transmission Identifier.
Transaction data files in XML format that defines each transaction and any corresponding documents and field data. Create one data file per transaction.
Unique 4 digit number for each trading partner
3 character abbreviation of the BOFA physical location
Sequential number within a transmission for each data file.
Trading partner populated value for internal tracking.
Text in memo line of the check
Amount to be paid in US dollars
Variable length of digits representing the 3of9 barcode on the stub
Name as it appears on credit card
There must be a corresponding document for every page defined in the metadata.
Designates front or back of the document.
The digital document component of the package transmission. An “electronic check”
must meet the legal definition of an “electronic image of, and electronic information derived from, a paper check.” The preferred document format is JPEG at 300 DPI

After Bank of America validates each transaction from a trading partner, an acknowledgement will be sent for each transaction. A negative acknowledgement with contain additional information about the failure(s).
All transaction acknowledgements from the incoming transmission are packaged together and sent using DTS. This is to help limit the number of files pushed to DTS for processing.

Each transmission uses the same base name as the transmission package even when the format does not meet the specification. This is to maintain linkage between the transmission received and the acknowledgment.
Uses the same base name as the transaction metadata. When the acknowledgement contains errors, the file extension will be prefixed by ‘n’.
JSON file indicating the package status by Bank of America. May contain zero or more errors.

When a transmission contains file level errors, transaction processing is halted. A single dummy transaction (negative) acknowledgement will be generated. The dummy values are fixed constants as documented.
